frozen_components: decoder backbone during initial GPT pretraining
architecture.summary: InstructRetro is a 48B-parameter auto-regressive language model built by continued pretraining of a 43B GPT backbone with retrieval-augmented pretraining (Retro), followed by instruction tuning. During pretraining, a Retro encoder (2-layer bidirectional transformer) encodes retrieved neighbors from a 1.2T-token database via BERT embeddings, and chunk-wise cross-attention layers fuse retrieved information into the decoder. During instruction tuning and inference, the encoder is gated off, leaving a decoder-only model that can operate with or without retrieved context. The key innovation is unfreezing decoder parameters during continued retrieval-augmented pretraining (unlike prior Retro-fitting that froze them), yielding better perplexity and downstream task performance after instruction tuning.

biblio.title: InstructRetro: Instruction Tuning post Retrieval-Augmented Pretraining

claims.dataset: validation set (1% held-out from pretraining corpus)
claims.statement: Retro 48B largely outperforms the counterpart GPT 43B trained on 1.2T tokens in terms of perplexity with only 2.58% additional GPU hours

claims.dataset: NQ, TriviaQA, NewsQA, SQuAD 1.1, SQuAD 2.0, Quoref, NarrativeQA, DROP
claims.statement: The average improvement of InstructRetro 43B is 7% over its GPT counterpart across 8 short-form QA and reading comprehension tasks
InstructRetro 43B achieves 10% average improvement over GPT across 4 challenging long-form QA tasks
InstructRetro 43B achieves 16% average improvement over GPT across 3 summarization tasks

claims.dataset: validation set (1% held-out from pretraining corpus)
claims.statement: Retro 48B achieves perplexity comparable to GPT models with 4 larger parameter sizes

claims.dataset: short-form QA, long-form QA, summarization tasks
claims.statement: One can ablate the encoder from InstructRetro architecture and directly use its decoder backbone, while achieving comparable results
Unfreezing decoder parameters during Retro-fitting converges faster and demonstrates better validation perplexity than freezing decoder parameters
Retrieval-augmented pretraining benefits saturate at scale without instruction tuning, but instruction tuning further unveils the potential of retrieval augmentation even when model size scales up
